channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Mali Defence Minister Killed in Coordinated Rebel Attacks
item.description: Sadio Camara died in a suicide truck bombing on his residence near Bamako as jihadist militants and separatist groups launched coordinated attacks across Mali. The assault marks the largest coordinated jihadist attack on Mali in years.

item.title: Seven Dead in Major Russian Attack on Ukraine
item.description: At least seven people were killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. The attack involved over 600 drones and missiles, one of the largest in days.

item.title: Five Pro-Palestine Activists Face Trial for Elbit Factory Raid in Germany
item.description: The trial of five European activists accused of attacking a factory linked to Israeli weapons manufacturer Elbit Systems begins in Germany. Prosecutors seek lengthy sentences, calling it a criminal organization act.

item.title: Car Bomb Explodes Outside Northern Ireland Police Station
item.description: A car bomb attack on a police station in Dunmurry, near Belfast, has been condemned as an attempt to undermine the 1998 Good Friday Agreement. Dissident Republican groups are suspected.

item.title: Israelis Question Netanyahu Leadership as Iran War Goals Unfulfilled
item.description: With Iran's government still standing and Hezbollah and Hamas not defeated, many Israelis express dissatisfaction with Netanyahu's wartime leadership ahead of elections scheduled for later this year.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade strikes in Lebanon as Iran truce talks stall
item.description: Ceasefires in Lebanon and the Iran-US conflict remain shaky as military attacks flare and direct negotiations between Washington and Tehran to end their war have stalled, with Iranian negotiators returning to Pakistan.

item.title: Can King Charles help heal the US-British rupture?
item.description: Not since Queen Elizabeth II traveled to Washington after the Suez Crisis has a British monarch's visit come at such a fraught time in Anglo-American relations, with the King seeking to restore strained ties.

item.title: Iran Offers Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz While Deferring Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iran has proposed a new deal to the US through Pakistani mediators that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for postponing nuclear negotiations to a later phase. The diplomatic outreach comes as Iranian FM Araghchi meets with Putin in St. Petersburg amid stalled US-Iran peace talks.

item.title: Iranian FM Meets Putin in Russia as Diplomatic Efforts Continue
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi traveled to Russia for talks with President Vladimir Putin, coordinating with Moscow after the war with Israel and the US. The visit follows stops in Pakistan and Oman aimed at restarting stalled ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Iran Submits Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has submitted a new proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, postponing nuclear negotiations for a later stage, Axios reported citing US officials.

item.title: 20,000 Seafarers Stranded in Persian Gulf as Hormuz Remains Blocked
item.description: Approximately 20,000 seafarers on hundreds of vessels remain stranded in the Persian Gulf, unable to cross the effectively closed Strait of Hormuz. The UN reports at least 10 seafarers have been killed since the conflict began.

item.title: Four Candidates Vie to Succeed UN Secretary-General Guterres
item.description: Michelle Bachelet (Chile), Rafael Grossi (Argentina), Rebeca Grynspan (Costa Rica), and Macky Sall (Senegal) presented their visions to lead the UN, focusing on restoring global peace and reforming the institution.

item.title: North Korea Opens Memorial Museum for Troops Killed in Ukraine War
item.description: North Korea inaugurated a memorial museum in Pyongyang honoring soldiers killed fighting for Russia, with Kim Jong Un attending alongside Russian officials including Defense Minister Andrei Beloushov. Russia and North Korea agreed to long-term military cooperation covering 2027-2031.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Pledges Full Support for Russia, Agrees Long-Term Military Cooperation
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un pledged continued support for Russia during talks with Russian Defense Minister Beloushov, agreeing to place military cooperation on a stable long-term footing through 2027-2031. The agreement comes as North Korea confirms sending troops to aid Russia in Ukraine.

item.title: Iran War Taking Toll on Chinese Manufacturing Economy
item.description: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on Chinese factory orders, costs and jobs as the Strait of Hormuz disruption hits exports. Costs have risen around 20% for some traders, adding strain to an economy already dealing with US tariffs and slower growth.

item.title: Erdogan Offers Support to Trump After White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
item.description: Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan called President Donald Trump to offer support following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents Association dinner. A gunman opened fire near the event attended by Trump and senior officials.

item.title: Iran FM Araghchi heads to Moscow as US-Iran peace talks stall
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to meet President Vladimir Putin in St. Petersburg on April 27 after diplomatic efforts in Pakistan and Oman failed to advance peace negotiations with the US.

item.title: Nearly 214,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine identified by media investigation
item.description: Mediazona and BBC Russian service have confirmed the identities of 213,858 Russian military personnel killed in Ukraine, with over 5,000 names added since early April. Actual figures are likely significantly higher.

item.title: Chinese EVs surge in South Korea with 33% market share as fuel prices climb
item.description: China-made electric vehicles now account for one in three new registrations in South Korea, up from 4.7% in 2022, driven by Tesla Shanghai models and competitive pricing amid elevated fuel costs linked to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan After Iranian FM Departs
item.description: President Trump canceled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushners planned trip to Islamabad for Iran peace talks, suggesting negotiations continue by phone instead.

item.title: King Charles State Visit to US to Proceed Despite Washington Shooting
item.description: Buckingham Palace confirmed King Charles and Queen Camilla's four-day state visit to the United States will proceed as planned starting Monday, following discussions with US officials after a shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner attended by President Trump.

item.title: Asia May Emerge as Unexpected Beneficiary from Iran War Fallout
item.description: While Asia has been highly vulnerable to the Iran war due to its dependence on Middle East energy, analysts suggest the region could emerge as a long-term winner from trends accelerated by the conflict, including supply chain diversification and energy transition.

item.title: China Uses Trade Truce With US to Expand Economic Leverage Before Summit
item.description: Beijing has used the trade truce with Washington to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain controls, and critical technology restrictions, expanding its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a potential summit between the nations next month.

item.title: Global Military Spending Hits Record $2.9 Trillion Despite US Cuts
item.description: World military expenditure rose 2.9% in 2025 to a record high, with the US accounting for less as Trump halted Ukraine aid while other nations ramped up defense budgets amid rising global tensions.

item.title: Security Under Review After Gunman Breaches White House Correspondents Dinner
item.description: Law enforcement officials are reassessing security protocols after an armed suspect was able to get close to an event attended by President Trump and senior officials, raising questions about protection arrangements.
